vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1791: foreman-independent tiered execution planning artifacts only. PAN-1762's durable foreman is still open, so this plan implements configuration, plan-time tier scheduling, relevance-map generation, and persisted readback artifacts without adding a second runtime loop.

plan.title: Tiered execution: difficulty-routed bead dispatch with plan-filtered commit-feed artifacts
plan.author: agent:gpt-5

narratives.Problem: PAN-1791's full design depends on PAN-1762's durable foreman. PAN-1762 is still open and docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md marks it operator-held at proposed, so implementing PAN-1791's runtime foreman loop now would create a second bead loop/status writer/commit dispatcher and violate the PRD's D2 rule: extend PAN-1762's foreman, do not design a second loop.
narratives.Proposal: Implement only the foreman-independent pieces now: (1) the disabled-by-default tiered_execution config contract and validation, (2) a pure plan-time tier scheduler that maps vBRIEF items through the tier table into deterministic tier runs, (3) a pure plan-time relevance-map builder using blocks edges, metadata.foundationFor, and files_scope overlap, (4) persistence/readback of the schedule and relevance map as plan metadata, and (5) explicit documentation/markers that runtime standing agents, supervisor dispatch, replay, pilot measurement, and commit/feed delivery are blocked by PAN-1762.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No durable foreman implementation in PAN-1791.
- No new bead loop, statusOverrides writer, commit runner, or supervisor dispatch loop.
- No standing tier-agent lifecycle, respawn, replay, or deliverAgentMessage feed delivery.
- No auto-start, merge, or runtime scheduling behavior changes.
- No hardcoded claude-code harness selection for CLIProxy-routed models; provider default routing remains authoritative.
narratives.Constraints: All code must be pure planning/config substrate unless the PRD explicitly lists it in the foreman-independent slice. Missing or overlapping difficulty mappings fail validation at plan time. Items missing metadata.difficulty fail schedule construction with an actionable diagnostic. Specs on main remain immutable after planning; any runtime status later still belongs in workspace continue.json statusOverrides.

autoDecisions.summary: Choose option B from the 2026-06-30 kickoff.
autoDecisions.rationale: Planning only the foreman-independent slices preserves PAN-1791's architecture and avoids inventing a second runtime loop while PAN-1762 remains open.
plan.metadata already supports Overdeck extensions such as canonicalFilename. A single nested key keeps the schedule/relevance artifact durable without changing vBRIEF's top-level shape.

autoDecisions.summary: Use pure libraries for scheduler and relevance map.
autoDecisions.rationale: The artifacts must be deterministic, unit-testable, and safe to consume later from PAN-1762's foreman without touching agents, tmux, git status, bead status, or delivery transports.
The runtime implementation is blocked by PAN-1762, so configuration support must not change current execution behavior until a later issue wires the foreman.

blockedUntilPan1762: FR-3 standing tier-agent lifecycle and feed delivery

items.title: Add disabled-by-default tiered_execution config schema and validation

narrative.Action: Extend the existing Cloister configuration surface with a `tiered_execution` section whose default is `enabled: false`. Define tiers as named entries with `model`, optional/provider-resolved `harness`, and `difficulties`; define `supervisor.subscribe` as `all | flagged | sampled`; preserve existing `model_selection.complexity_routing` behavior. Validation must require every vBRIEF difficulty (`trivial`, `simple`, `medium`, `complex`, `expert`) to map to exactly one tier when enabled, reject overlaps and gaps by name, reject unknown supervisor policies, and apply existing harness/provider policy checks instead of hardcoding `claude-code` for CLIProxy-routed models. No runtime execution path may read this config to spawn or dispatch agents in this issue.

items.title: Default loaded config contains tiered_execution.enabled === false and existing non-tiered execution behavior remains unchanged.
Validation accepts a complete non-overlapping difficulty mapping and rejects missing, duplicate, or unknown difficulty entries with messages naming the offending difficulty.
Validation rejects unknown supervisor.subscribe values and unsafe harness/provider combinations using the existing harness policy surface.
No work-agent spawn, foreman loop, tmux, deliverAgentMessage, git status, or statusOverrides path is introduced or modified by this bead.
Create pure plan-time tier scheduler

narrative.Action: Add a pure library, likely `src/lib/vbrief/tier-scheduler.ts`, that accepts a parsed `VBriefDocument` plus the validated tier table and returns an ordered schedule artifact. Reuse `groupItemsByWave(doc)` from `src/lib/vbrief/dag.ts` for DAG order, map each actionable item by `metadata.difficulty`, and coalesce consecutive same-tier items into tier runs. The function must be deterministic, side-effect free, and must fail with actionable diagnostics for missing `metadata.difficulty` or an unroutable difficulty. Do not spawn agents, do not update vBRIEF status, and do not read live beads or git state.

items.title: Given a valid plan and tier table, the scheduler returns stable ordered tier runs containing item IDs and tier names in DAG/wave order.
Consecutive actionable items assigned to the same tier are coalesced into one run, while tier changes create a new run.
Missing metadata.difficulty and unroutable difficulty values fail before runtime with diagnostics naming the item ID and difficulty.
The scheduler has no dependencies on agent state, tmux, bd, git, statusOverrides, or deliverAgentMessage.
Create pure plan-time relevance-map builder

narrative.Action: Add a pure relevance-map builder, likely `src/lib/vbrief/tier-relevance-map.ts`, that consumes the parsed plan plus the scheduler artifact and returns, for each completed/source item, which future tier runs or items need to ingest that source commit and why. A source item is relevant when it blocks a future item by `blocks` reachability, appears in `metadata.foundationFor`, or overlaps a future item's `files_scope` using the existing `hasFileOverlap()` behavior from `src/lib/vbrief/dag.ts`. Preserve reason details in the artifact, including the source item, destination item, tier, and reason (`blocks`, `foundationFor`, `files_scope`). Do not construct commit diffs or deliver messages; this is a plan-time map only.

items.title: The builder records blocks-edge relevance from source items to downstream tier-assigned items, including transitive dependency reachability where the DAG says the downstream item cannot start before the source chain completes.
The builder records metadata.foundationFor relevance with a distinct reason and ignores unknown foundationFor IDs only by returning a named validation diagnostic.
The builder records files_scope overlap relevance using the existing hasFileOverlap semantics and includes the overlapping scope information in the reason payload.
Items with no relevant future tier work produce empty relevance entries rather than runtime deliveries.
Persist and read the tier schedule plus relevance map as plan metadata

narrative.Action: Define a stable artifact shape under `plan.metadata.tieredExecution` containing the validated tier table snapshot or tier names, ordered `runs`, and the relevance map with reason payloads. Wire read/write helpers so planning/finalization code can attach the artifact to the proposed vBRIEF and callers can read it without recomputation. Preserve vBRIEF's two top-level key shape and existing metadata fields such as canonicalFilename. Tests should round-trip a spec with the artifact and confirm unrelated metadata survives. No runtime status field, workspace continue.json, bead status, or dashboard live-agent state should be written by this bead.

items.title: A proposed vBRIEF can include plan.metadata.tieredExecution with runs and relevance-map entries, and JSON read/write round-trips preserve the artifact exactly.
Existing plan.metadata fields, especially canonicalFilename, survive artifact writes unchanged.
The artifact is read-only planning data for PAN-1791 and does not update plan.status, item status, continue.json statusOverrides, beads, tmux sessions, or agent state.
docs/VBRIEF.md documents the plan.metadata.tieredExecution extension and states that runtime consumption is blocked by PAN-1762.
Mark runtime tiered-execution slices blocked on PAN-1762

narrative.Action: Keep PAN-1791's PRD and spec explicit that runtime tiered execution remains blocked until PAN-1762 lands. If follow-up tracker work is created, it must be blocked by PAN-1762 and must consume the persisted plan artifact instead of creating a parallel foreman loop. Mention the blocked runtime slices by name: foreman bureaucracy ownership, standing tier-agent lifecycle, supervisor commit-event dispatch, replay, deliverAgentMessage feed delivery, and pilot cost measurement.

items.title: The PRD and vBRIEF both state that PAN-1791 implements only foreman-independent planning/config artifacts while PAN-1762 is open.
The runtime follow-up list explicitly names the blocked slices and says they must extend PAN-1762's foreman rather than adding a second loop.
No issue comment, doc, or spec wording implies that PAN-1791 may spawn standing agents, dispatch supervisor events, run replay, or write runtime status before PAN-1762 lands.
